survey_title: Assignment Feedback Application and Skill Development Tracking Survey
survey_description: This survey assesses how effectively you apply assignment feedback and track your own skill development. Answer all 20 questions honestly. Lower scores indicate stronger feedback engagement; higher scores indicate avoidance or disengagement.

questions.text: When you receive written feedback on an assignment, how thoroughly do you read through all the comments?

options.label: Always — I read every comment carefully
Rarely — I check the grade and move on
Never — I do not read feedback comments

questions.text: How well do you understand the feedback comments you receive from instructors?

options.label: Very well — I always understand what is meant
Poorly — I often find comments confusing

questions.text: How often do you apply specific feedback points when working on your next assignment?

options.label: Always — I address every point raised
Rarely — I seldom change my approach

questions.text: How often do you make a written note of feedback points to refer back to later?
After receiving feedback, how often do you reflect on why you made the errors identified?

options.label: Always — I analyse each mistake carefully
Never — I do not reflect on my errors

questions.text: How confident are you that you can identify the specific areas of your work that need improvement?
How often do you compare feedback from different assignments to identify recurring weaknesses?
How often do you ask your instructor to clarify feedback comments you do not understand?

options.label: Always — I seek clarification whenever needed
Rarely — I prefer not to ask

questions.text: How accurately can you assess the quality of your own work before receiving feedback?

options.label: Very accurately — my self-assessment closely matches the grade
Moderately — I sometimes misjudge my work
Poorly — my self-assessment is often wrong

questions.text: How often do you use feedback to set specific improvement goals for your next piece of work?
How clearly do you track your skill improvement from one assignment to the next?

options.label: Very clearly — I keep a log or record of progress
Somewhat — I notice improvement but do not track it
Not clearly — I rarely notice whether I am improving

questions.text: How often do you feel motivated to improve after reading your feedback?
How often do you review your previous assignments alongside the feedback before starting a new one?
How effectively do you translate general feedback comments into specific actions?

options.label: Very effectively — I always know what to do next
Moderately — I sometimes struggle to act on general comments
Very poorly — general feedback means nothing to me

questions.text: How often do you discuss your feedback with a classmate or study peer?
How often do you feel frustrated or defensive when you receive critical feedback?

options.label: Never — I always accept critical feedback calmly
Always — critical feedback always upsets me

questions.text: How often do you proactively ask for additional feedback beyond what is provided?
How well do you remember and apply feedback from assignments completed more than a month ago?

options.label: Partially — I recall some but forget most

questions.text: How often do you feel the feedback you receive helps you understand what is expected at university level?
How often do you notice a clear improvement in your grades as a result of applying feedback?

options.label: Always — applying feedback consistently improves my results

scoring.label: Highly Feedback-Literate
scoring.description: Outstanding feedback engagement. You read, apply, and track all feedback consistently and proactively seek more. No intervention needed.

scoring.label: Strong Feedback Engagement
scoring.description: You apply most feedback well and show good self-regulation. Minor gaps in tracking or reflection. Continue and expand current habits.

scoring.label: Developing Feedback Engagement
scoring.description: You read feedback but apply it inconsistently. Consider keeping a feedback log and setting improvement goals after each assignment.

scoring.label: Passive Feedback Receiver
scoring.description: You acknowledge feedback without acting on it deliberately. Seek clarification from instructors and discuss feedback with peers.

scoring.label: Feedback-Reluctant
scoring.description: You rarely apply feedback and may find criticism discouraging. Attend feedback workshops and develop a structured review habit.

scoring.label: Significantly Feedback-Avoidant
scoring.description: Feedback is largely ignored or causes strong negative reactions. Academic support or mentoring is strongly recommended.

scoring.label: Critical Feedback Disengagement
scoring.description: Almost no feedback is read or applied. Immediate intervention needed. Please speak with your personal tutor urgently.
